critic_raw:
Issue 1 — Behavioral Outcome Scope Jump
The evaluation report claims the model will 'reduce misinformation spread.' The evidence presented is offline precision on a hand-labeled holdout set measuring whether posts violate policy. These are two different things. Policy-violation detection measures whether a post contains content that violates rules. Spread reduction requires that (a) the model identifies posts before they spread widely, (b) flagged posts are removed or down-ranked quickly enough to limit reach, (c) users do not migrate to evasive phrasing, and (d) the model covers the channels through which spread actually occurs. None of (a)-(d) is measured. The conclusion names the wrong outcome — it should say 'content review aid' not 'misinformation spread reducer.'

Issue 2 — Workflow Dependence of Effect
The claimed effect (reduced spread) depends entirely on how moderators and platform systems use the model's output. The report notes the classifier outperforms the keyword filter at offline precision. But spread reduction requires real-time flagging, moderator capacity, removal latency, and coverage of private groups — none of which are analyzed. The model could have perfect offline precision and reduce spread by zero if the operational workflow does not act on flags quickly enough or does not cover the channels where spread concentrates. The report does not analyze any of these workflow factors.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 — Analysis:
Issue 1: The scope jump is explicit and acknowledged in the report description: 'no evidence is presented about how moderators will use model outputs, whether flagged posts will be removed quickly enough, whether users will migrate to evasive phrasing, or whether harmful spread is concentrated in private groups.' This is the report itself identifying the gap. The defender cannot argue the claim is supported when the report's own description lists these four missing evidence items.

Issue 2: Workflow dependence is the mechanism by which the scope jump manifests. Better offline precision does not translate to spread reduction without the operational pathway being in place and effective. The report presents no workflow analysis.

Pass 2 — Verdicts: Both conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_moderation_308 — Isolated Debate Run 1

Both issues conceded. The report's own description acknowledges the four missing evidence items that would be needed to support the spread-reduction claim. The scope jump from policy-violation detection to misinformation spread reduction is clear.

Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
